title: tldraw SDK Onboarding
description: A guided tour of the tldraw monorepo — an infinite canvas SDK for React. Learn the architecture, core packages, shape system, and how everything fits together.

steps.title: Monorepo overview
steps.description: Welcome to the tldraw monorepo! The codebase is organized into three top-level directories: `packages/` contains the core SDK libraries, `apps/` houses the web applications and extensions, and `templates/` provides starter projects for different frameworks. The build system uses LazyRepo for incremental builds and Yarn Berry workspaces for dependency management.

interactiveActions.description: Explore the top-level directory structure to get oriented

steps.title: The editor engine
steps.description: The `@tldraw/editor` package is the foundational infinite canvas engine. It provides the core `Editor` class, reactive state management via signals, the shape system (ShapeUtil), tools as state machines (StateNode), and the bindings system for shape relationships. Crucially, it contains NO shapes, tools, or UI — it's a pure engine that the higher-level `tldraw` package builds upon. The companion `@tldraw/driver` package provides an imperative API for driving the editor programmatically.

interactiveActions.description: Open the main Editor class to see the core API surface

steps.title: The batteries-included SDK
steps.description: The `@tldraw/tldraw` package builds on the editor to provide a complete, ready-to-use canvas experience. It includes default shape utilities (arrow, draw, geo, text, note, image, video, and more), a full tool set (select, hand, eraser, laser, zoom), and a responsive UI system with customizable components. This is what most developers import when using tldraw.

interactiveActions.description: Hover over the shapes directory to see all built-in shape types

steps.title: State, store, and schema
steps.description: Three packages form tldraw's reactive data layer. `@tldraw/state` provides the signals system (Atom, Computed) for reactive state with automatic dependency tracking. `@tldraw/store` is a reactive client-side database with IndexedDB persistence and migration support. `@tldraw/tlschema` defines the type definitions, validators, and migrations for all shapes, bindings, and records.

interactiveActions.description: Hover over the state package to see the signals implementation

steps.title: Multiplayer sync
steps.description: The `@tldraw/sync` and `@tldraw/sync-core` packages provide WebSocket-based real-time collaboration. They handle conflict resolution, presence, and document synchronization. The `sync-cloudflare` template demonstrates a production-ready multiplayer setup using Cloudflare Durable Objects.

interactiveActions.description: Explore the sync-core package for the multiplayer protocol implementation

steps.title: Applications
steps.description: The `apps/` directory contains the applications built with tldraw. `examples/` is the primary SDK showcase and development playground. `dotcom/` is the tldraw.com production application with its client frontend, sync-worker backend, and supporting workers. `docs/` powers the documentation site at tldraw.dev. `vscode/` is the VS Code extension. `mcp-app/` provides Model Context Protocol integration for AI-powered workflows.

interactiveActions.description: Hover over the examples app — this is where most SDK development happens

steps.title: Starter templates
steps.description: The `templates/` directory provides ready-to-use starter projects for different frameworks and use cases. There are templates for Vite, Next.js, Vue, and more specialized setups like agent-based workflows, chat interfaces, multiplayer sync with Cloudflare, shader experiments, image pipelines, and branching chat workflows. Use `npx create-tldraw` to scaffold a new project from these templates.

interactiveActions.description: Browse the available templates to find one matching your use case

steps.title: Putting it all together
steps.description: You've now seen all the major areas of tldraw! The architecture flows from `@tldraw/state` (signals) → `@tldraw/store` (reactive database) → `@tldraw/editor` (canvas engine) → `@tldraw/tldraw` (full SDK). Applications in `apps/` consume the SDK, and `templates/` help new users get started. To begin developing, run `yarn dev` to start the examples app. Key commands: `yarn test run` (tests), `yarn typecheck` (type checking), `yarn lint` (linting).

interactiveActions.description: Freely explore the codebase — you now know where everything lives!
